evals.prompt: Usa el dev-workflow para agregar un comando 'uninstall' al CLI que elimine los archivos del skill de la ubicación global o local. Debe preguntar al usuario cuál quiere desinstalar si ambas existen.
evals.expected_output: Should dispatch Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool. The subagent produces SPEC.md inside docs/workflow/<feature-name>/. The orchestrator presents the subagent's summary to the user for review. Phase 1 does NOT create a branch. Should ask clarifying questions about edge cases before writing the spec.

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Identifies the task as Standard complexity (not Simple)

assertions.name: starts-at-phase-1
assertions.description: Begins at Phase 1 (SPEC), not Phase 2

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool instead of executing inline

assertions.name: orchestrator-stays-minimal
assertions.description: The orchestrator only handles phase detection, dispatch, and review — does not explore code or write files directly

assertions.name: subagent-explores-codebase
assertions.description: The subagent (not the orchestrator) explores existing CLI code (bin/, src/) before writing spec

assertions.name: asks-clarifying-questions
assertions.description: Clarifying questions are surfaced to the user — either the subagent returns them or the orchestrator presents them

assertions.name: produces-spec-md
assertions.description: Produces a SPEC.md with Purpose, Use Cases, Requirements, Edge Cases, Acceptance Criteria, Out of Scope

assertions.name: no-branch-created
assertions.description: Does NOT create a git branch (that happens in Phase 2)

assertions.name: creates-workflow-directory
assertions.description: Creates docs/workflow/<feature-name>/ directory

assertions.name: presents-summary-for-review
assertions.description: The orchestrator presents the subagent's return summary to the user for approval

evals.prompt: Quiero usar SDD para hacer un fix — el comando 'status' no muestra la versión del skill instalado, solo dice 'active'. Debería mostrar también la versión que tiene instalada el usuario comparada con la última disponible en npm.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as 'simple', skip Phase 1, dispatch Phase 2 to a subagent. The subagent creates both docs/workflow/<name>/ and feature/<name> with the SAME name. Must produce PLAN.md with tests-first task ordering. The orchestrator presents the summary for review.

assertions.name: classifies-as-simple
assertions.description: Classifies the task as Simple complexity

assertions.name: skips-phase-1
assertions.description: Skips Phase 1 (SPEC), goes directly to Phase 2

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 2 to a subagent via the Agent tool instead of executing inline

assertions.name: orchestrator-stays-minimal
assertions.description: The orchestrator only handles complexity triage, dispatch, and review — does not write PLAN.md directly

assertions.name: branch-matches-workflow-name
assertions.description: Branch name and workflow directory use the SAME feature-name

assertions.name: creates-feature-branch
assertions.description: Creates a feature/<name> branch

assertions.name: produces-plan-md
assertions.description: Produces a PLAN.md with Architecture Decisions, File Map, and Tasks

assertions.name: tasks-ordered-tests-first
assertions.description: Tasks in the plan are ordered with test tasks before implementation tasks

assertions.name: presents-summary-for-review
assertions.description: The orchestrator presents the subagent's return summary to the user for approval

evals.prompt: I already have a SPEC.md and PLAN.md in docs/workflow/add-dry-run-flag/. The plan has 3 tasks. I've finished tasks 1 and 2 (tests pass). I'm on branch feature/add-dry-run-flag. Continue the workflow from where I left off — run /dev-workflow
evals.expected_output: Should detect Phase 3. Dispatch a subagent for task 3 only (not the whole phase). The subagent runs RED-GREEN-REFACTOR for task 3 and returns results. The orchestrator presents results, commits after approval, then proceeds to Phase 4 via another subagent.

assertions.name: detects-phase-3
assertions.description: Correctly detects the workflow is in Phase 3 (IMPLEMENT)

assertions.name: verifies-correct-branch
assertions.description: Verifies it is on branch feature/add-dry-run-flag, not main

assertions.name: identifies-completed-tasks
assertions.description: Identifies tasks 1 and 2 as already completed

assertions.name: dispatches-per-task-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches a subagent for task 3 ONLY — one subagent per task, not one for the whole phase

assertions.name: subagent-does-not-commit
assertions.description: The Phase 3 subagent does NOT commit — it returns results and the orchestrator commits after user approval

assertions.name: orchestrator-commits-after-approval
assertions.description: The orchestrator performs the git commit after presenting results and receiving user approval

assertions.name: proceeds-to-phase-4
assertions.description: Advances to Phase 4 (VERIFY) after task 3 is done

assertions.name: phase-4-dispatched-as-subagent
assertions.description: Phase 4 is also dispatched to a subagent, not executed inline

assertions.name: asks-about-milestone
assertions.description: Asks about milestone assignment in Phase 4 (project uses milestones per CLAUDE.md)

evals.prompt: Usa el dev-workflow para agregar validación de inputs al comando 'init'. Si el directorio destino no existe, debe mostrar un error claro. Si ya tiene un skill instalado, preguntar si quiere sobreescribir.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as standard. Dispatch Phase 1 to a subagent. If the subagent cannot resolve ambiguity, it should return clarifying questions instead of a completed SPEC.md. The orchestrator collects answers and dispatches a NEW subagent (not the same one).

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Identifies the task as Standard complexity

assertions.name: dispatches-phase-1-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: subagent-returns-questions
assertions.description: If ambiguity exists, the subagent returns clarifying questions in its summary instead of writing an incomplete SPEC.md

assertions.name: new-subagent-for-answers
assertions.description: After collecting answers, the orchestrator dispatches a NEW subagent with the answers — does not re-enter the previous subagent

assertions.name: orchestrator-never-writes-files
assertions.description: The orchestrator does not write SPEC.md or any workflow files directly — only subagents write files

assertions.name: abort-handled-by-orchestrator
assertions.description: If the user says 'abort' at any point, the orchestrator handles it directly without dispatching a subagent

evals.prompt: Estoy en Phase 3 de un workflow con 5 tasks. El subagente del task 3 falló — los tests pasan pero rompió los tests del task 2. ¿Qué debería hacer el orquestador?
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator should detect the regression from the subagent's return summary, report the failure to the user, and offer options: retry with a new subagent (including guidance about the regression), skip the task, or abort. It should NOT attempt to fix the regression itself.

assertions.name: detects-regression-from-summary
assertions.description: The orchestrator reads the subagent's return summary which reports the regression

assertions.name: reports-failure-to-user
assertions.description: The orchestrator presents the regression details to the user

assertions.name: offers-retry-options
assertions.description: Offers retry (new subagent with guidance), retry with different approach, skip task, or abort

assertions.name: dispatches-new-subagent-on-retry
assertions.description: If user chooses retry, dispatches a NEW subagent with the regression context — does not re-enter the failed subagent

assertions.name: orchestrator-does-not-fix-directly
assertions.description: The orchestrator does not attempt to fix code or tests directly — it delegates to a subagent

evals.prompt: El usuario pide cambios después de que el subagente de Phase 2 entrega el PLAN.md. El usuario dice: 'no me gusta la arquitectura, quiero usar un patrón diferente para el task 2'. ¿Qué hace el orquestador?
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator should dispatch a NEW subagent with the user's feedback plus the current PLAN.md state. It should NOT edit the PLAN.md directly or re-enter the previous subagent.

assertions.name: dispatches-new-subagent-with-feedback
assertions.description: Dispatches a new subagent with the user's feedback and the current PLAN.md path

assertions.name: does-not-edit-directly
assertions.description: The orchestrator does NOT edit PLAN.md directly — a subagent handles modifications

assertions.name: does-not-reenter-previous-subagent
assertions.description: Does NOT attempt to continue the previous Phase 2 subagent — dispatches a fresh one

assertions.name: includes-current-artifact-state
assertions.description: The new subagent receives the path to the existing PLAN.md so it can modify in place rather than starting from scratch

assertions.name: presents-updated-summary
assertions.description: After the new subagent finishes, the orchestrator presents the updated summary for another review cycle

evals.prompt: Run /dev-workflow — I need to add support for YAML config files alongside the existing JSON config. Users should be able to use either format. This is a big feature with schema validation, migration tooling, and documentation updates.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as standard (multi-file, domain logic). Dispatch Phase 1. The subagent should explore the existing config handling code, identify the JSON config structure, and produce a SPEC.md covering both formats, migration path, and schema validation.

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Identifies the task as Standard complexity due to multi-file changes and domain logic

assertions.name: starts-at-phase-1
assertions.description: Begins at Phase 1 (SPEC) — does not skip to Phase 2

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: reads-claude-md
assertions.description: The subagent reads CLAUDE.md to detect project conventions before producing the spec

assertions.name: produces-spec-md
assertions.description: Produces a SPEC.md with clear acceptance criteria and edge cases

assertions.name: no-branch-created
assertions.description: Phase 1 does NOT create a git branch

assertions.name: presents-review-gate
assertions.description: Presents the review gate format with status, summary, files, and next phase prompt

evals.prompt: Quiero abortar el workflow. Estoy a mitad del Phase 3, task 2 de 4 ya está committed. Ya no quiero esta feature. /dev-workflow abort
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator should handle the abort directly (never delegate abort to a subagent). Should ask for confirmation and offer 3 options: pause, abort keep branch, full abort. Should list what will be removed.

assertions.name: orchestrator-handles-abort-directly
assertions.description: The orchestrator handles the abort protocol directly — does NOT dispatch a subagent for abort

assertions.name: asks-confirmation
assertions.description: Asks the user for confirmation before proceeding with abort

assertions.name: offers-three-options
assertions.description: Offers: pause (default), abort keep branch, full abort

assertions.name: lists-what-will-be-removed
assertions.description: Lists exactly what will be removed (workflow directory, branch) for each option

assertions.name: pause-is-default
assertions.description: Pause is presented as the default/recommended option

evals.prompt: Use SDD to add a --verbose flag to the install command. It's a small change — just pipe through debug output that's already there but suppressed.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as simple. Skip Phase 1, go to Phase 2. The plan should be minimal with 1-2 tasks. Branch and workflow directory should use a consistent feature name.

assertions.name: classifies-as-simple
assertions.description: Classifies as Simple complexity — single concern, small scope

assertions.name: skips-phase-1
assertions.description: Skips Phase 1 and goes directly to Phase 2 (PLAN)

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 2 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: produces-plan-md
assertions.description: Produces a PLAN.md with minimal tasks (1-2)

assertions.name: creates-feature-branch
assertions.description: Creates a feature/<name> branch — even simple tasks need a branch

assertions.name: plan-marks-simple
assertions.description: The PLAN.md header indicates Complexity: simple

evals.prompt: dev-workflow. Tengo dos directorios en docs/workflow/: add-caching/ y fix-timeout/. Ambos tienen PLAN.md. ¿Cuál continúo?
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator should detect the ambiguity (multiple workflow directories) and ask the user which feature to continue. It should NOT pick one automatically or dispatch a subagent before resolving the ambiguity.

assertions.name: detects-multiple-workflows
assertions.description: Detects that there are multiple workflow directories in docs/workflow/

assertions.name: asks-user-which-feature
assertions.description: Asks the user which feature they want to continue instead of picking one automatically

assertions.name: does-not-dispatch-before-resolution
assertions.description: Does NOT dispatch a subagent until the user has clarified which workflow to continue

assertions.name: lists-both-features
assertions.description: Lists both add-caching and fix-timeout as options for the user

evals.prompt: I want to use the dev-workflow to refactor the error handling across the entire CLI. Right now errors are thrown as raw strings, I want them to use a custom AppError class with error codes. This touches every command file.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as standard (multi-file refactor with domain logic). Phase 1 should explore existing error patterns and produce a spec covering the error class design, migration strategy, and which files are affected.

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Identifies as Standard — multi-file refactor with new domain concept

assertions.name: starts-at-phase-1
assertions.description: Begins at Phase 1 (SPEC) to define the AppError class design

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: subagent-explores-error-patterns
assertions.description: The subagent explores existing error handling patterns in the codebase before writing the spec

assertions.name: spec-covers-migration
assertions.description: The SPEC.md addresses migration strategy (how to transition from raw strings to AppError)

assertions.name: presents-summary-for-review
assertions.description: The orchestrator presents the subagent's summary for user review

evals.prompt: Run the dev-workflow. My project is a Python Flask app — it uses pytest for testing, ruff for linting, and the base branch is 'develop'. I want to add rate limiting to the /api/upload endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Should detect project settings from CLAUDE.md (or the user's description): pytest as test command, ruff for lint, develop as base branch. These settings should be passed to the subagent in the dispatch template.

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Identifies as Standard complexity — new feature with domain logic

assertions.name: detects-project-settings
assertions.description: Detects or acknowledges project-specific settings: pytest, ruff, develop branch

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: passes-settings-to-subagent
assertions.description: The dispatch template includes detected settings (test command, base branch, lint command)

assertions.name: starts-at-phase-1
assertions.description: Begins at Phase 1 (SPEC) for the rate limiting feature

evals.prompt: Usa /dev-workflow para... la verdad no estoy seguro de qué quiero. Algo con mejorar el rendimiento del CLI, se siente lento pero no sé dónde está el cuello de botella.
evals.expected_output: The request is vague. The orchestrator should still classify and start Phase 1, but the subagent should return clarifying questions rather than guessing at a spec. Questions should help narrow down: which commands are slow, what 'slow' means in numbers, whether there's profiling data, etc.

assertions.name: does-not-refuse
assertions.description: Does NOT refuse to start the workflow — vague requests are valid, Phase 1 handles ambiguity

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Classifies as Standard — performance work is inherently multi-faceted

assertions.name: dispatches-phase-1
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent

assertions.name: surfaces-clarifying-questions
assertions.description: Clarifying questions are surfaced to help narrow the scope (which commands, metrics, profiling)

assertions.name: does-not-write-spec-without-clarity
assertions.description: Does NOT produce a SPEC.md based on guesses — asks first

evals.prompt: Phase 3, task 1 of 3. The subagent finished but it wrote 0 tests — it went straight to implementation. The code works but there are no test files. What should happen?
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator should detect that the RED step was skipped (no tests written). It should report this to the user as an issue and offer to retry with a new subagent that emphasizes the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

assertions.name: detects-missing-tests
assertions.description: The orchestrator detects that no test files were created despite the project having a test runner

assertions.name: reports-violation-to-user
assertions.description: Reports to the user that the TDD cycle was not followed — RED step was skipped

assertions.name: offers-retry-with-guidance
assertions.description: Offers to retry with a new subagent that includes explicit guidance about writing tests first

assertions.name: does-not-approve-without-tests
assertions.description: Does NOT commit or approve the task without tests when the project has a test runner

assertions.name: orchestrator-does-not-write-tests
assertions.description: The orchestrator does not write the tests itself — it dispatches a new subagent

evals.prompt: dev-workflow para agregar un health check endpoint a mi API. El proyecto no tiene tests configurados — no hay test runner, no hay directorio de tests, nada. ¿Cómo adapta el workflow?
evals.expected_output: Should detect there's no test infrastructure. Phase 3 should adapt: instead of RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, use IMPLEMENT-VERIFY-REFACTOR. Should suggest (but not require) setting up a test framework. Acceptance criteria still need manual verification.

assertions.name: detects-no-test-runner
assertions.description: Detects that the project has no test infrastructure configured

assertions.name: adapts-phase-3
assertions.description: Acknowledges that Phase 3 will use IMPLEMENT-VERIFY-REFACTOR instead of RED-GREEN-REFACTOR

assertions.name: suggests-test-setup
assertions.description: Suggests setting up a minimal test framework but does not require it

assertions.name: acceptance-criteria-still-apply
assertions.description: Notes that acceptance criteria must still be verified even without automated tests

assertions.name: proceeds-with-workflow
assertions.description: Does NOT refuse to run the workflow — adapts instead

evals.prompt: Phase 4 — all 4 tasks are implemented, tests pass, I'm on branch feature/add-webhooks. SPEC.md and PLAN.md are in docs/workflow/add-webhooks/. Run /dev-workflow to finish up.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Phase 4 (VERIFY). Dispatch a subagent that runs lint, type checks, full test suite. Should clean up workflow artifacts (delete docs/workflow/add-webhooks/). Should create a PR or prepare a commit. Should ask about milestone assignment.

assertions.name: detects-phase-4
assertions.description: Correctly detects the workflow is in Phase 4 (VERIFY)

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 4 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: runs-full-verification
assertions.description: The subagent runs lint, type checks, and the full test suite

assertions.name: cleans-up-artifacts
assertions.description: Removes docs/workflow/add-webhooks/ (SPEC.md and PLAN.md are working documents, not permanent)

assertions.name: prepares-pr-or-commit
assertions.description: Creates a PR or prepares the final commit with proper conventional commit format

assertions.name: asks-about-milestone
assertions.description: Asks about milestone assignment (project uses milestones per CLAUDE.md)

evals.prompt: Quiero usar el workflow para renombrar el comando 'init' a 'setup' en todo el CLI. Es un rename simple — actualizar el comando, los tests, el help text, y el README.
evals.expected_output: Borderline simple/standard. Could go either way. The orchestrator should make a judgment call and explain why. If simple: skip to Phase 2. If standard: start Phase 1. Either way, the reasoning should be transparent.

assertions.name: makes-complexity-judgment
assertions.description: Makes an explicit complexity classification with reasoning

assertions.name: explains-reasoning
assertions.description: Explains why it classified the task as simple or standard — the reasoning is transparent

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches the appropriate phase to a subagent

assertions.name: branch-created-regardless
assertions.description: Regardless of complexity, a feature branch is created (in Phase 2)

assertions.name: consistent-naming
assertions.description: Branch name and workflow directory use the same feature-name

evals.prompt: I have SPEC.md in docs/workflow/add-caching/ approved. Run /dev-workflow to continue to Phase 2. I want the plan to have no more than 3 tasks — keep it tight.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Phase 2 (SPEC exists, no PLAN). Dispatch Phase 2 subagent. The user's constraint about max 3 tasks should be passed to the subagent as additional context.

assertions.name: detects-phase-2
assertions.description: Correctly detects Phase 2 — SPEC.md exists, no PLAN.md

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 2 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: passes-user-constraint
assertions.description: Passes the user's constraint (max 3 tasks) to the subagent in the dispatch prompt

assertions.name: creates-feature-branch
assertions.description: The subagent creates a feature/<name> branch

assertions.name: produces-plan-md
assertions.description: Produces a PLAN.md that respects the task count constraint

assertions.name: presents-review-gate
assertions.description: Presents the review gate for user approval before proceeding

evals.prompt: dev-workflow. The PLAN.md in docs/workflow/fix-logging/ has 2 tasks. Task 1 test files exist but have import errors — they don't compile. I'm on branch feature/fix-logging. Continue.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Phase 3. Should identify that task 1 has test files with errors — this means task 1 is NOT complete (broken tests = still in Phase 3). Should dispatch a subagent for task 1 to fix the tests and complete the implementation.

assertions.name: detects-phase-3
assertions.description: Correctly detects Phase 3 (IMPLEMENT)

assertions.name: identifies-broken-tests
assertions.description: Identifies that task 1's test files have errors — task 1 is incomplete

assertions.name: does-not-skip-broken-task
assertions.description: Does NOT skip task 1 or mark it as complete — broken tests mean it's still in progress

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent-for-task-1
assertions.description: Dispatches a subagent for task 1 to fix the tests and complete implementation

assertions.name: subagent-gets-error-context
assertions.description: The subagent receives context about the existing broken test files so it can fix them rather than start from scratch

evals.prompt: Use the dev-workflow to add internationalization (i18n) support to the CLI. All user-facing strings should be extractable. Support English and Spanish initially. This is a foundational change that touches almost every file.
evals.expected_output: Should classify as standard — this is a large, cross-cutting feature. Phase 1 should produce a thorough spec covering the i18n framework choice, string extraction strategy, fallback behavior, and which files are affected. The subagent should ask clarifying questions about framework preference.

assertions.name: classifies-as-standard
assertions.description: Identifies as Standard complexity — large cross-cutting feature

assertions.name: starts-at-phase-1
assertions.description: Begins at Phase 1 (SPEC) — too complex to skip

assertions.name: dispatches-subagent
assertions.description: Dispatches Phase 1 to a subagent via the Agent tool

assertions.name: subagent-explores-string-usage
assertions.description: The subagent explores existing user-facing strings in the codebase

assertions.name: surfaces-clarifying-questions
assertions.description: Surfaces clarifying questions about i18n framework, string format, locale detection strategy

assertions.name: presents-summary-for-review
assertions.description: Presents the review gate summary for user approval
